inline_comments.message: This is the internal DMI -> NCMP topic. Should be made generic like "my-topic-name"
We don't need to return the topic to the client

Contact Fiachra Corcoran regarding ONAP conventions.

Response was that there aren't any conventions to speak of but we would use dashes (i.e. my-new-topic) instead of dot notation (i.e. my.new.topic) for topic name as per point number 2 "https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/CPS-821+Spike%3A+Support+Async+read-write+operations+on+CPS-NCMP+interface#CPS821Spike:SupportAsyncreadwriteoperationsonCPSNCMPinterface-ProposedDesign"
Why has this changed? Is it due to the new uri builder?

Will this take care of 
* no topic in url    e.g. dmi/dostuff?options={...}
* null topic in url  e.g. dmi/dostuff?options={...}&topic=null
* empty topic in url e.g. dmi/dostuff?options={...}&topic=""
* missing topic in url e.g. dmi/dostuff?options={...}&topic=
If the base path does need to be modified then this should be 2022

Yes It can be handled by new code.

.pathSegment("{dmiBasePath}") -> /dmi
the convention for topic names is not determined this (our) REST API. I simply would say nothing about it here (your example is good enough)

I am always confused on how to format examples but I think for a single example it should be just:
example: my-topic-name

Check with Aditya and/or try out using Swagger UI
add a (new) test  with a topic

depending if we stil need 2 variations so separate form the one below:
someErrorMessage  or sampleErrorMessage
consider using .contains() might be good enough and require less contant and wil be less brittle
bit more detail: topicname for (triggering) async responses
should all this be in a new url-utility class (so we can also tets it independently) ?
I think teh original idea was to only accept 1 specific topic name, but maybe since teh scope (strimzi integration) has changed we need to udpate teh A/C of teh User Story!

sometimes I like to extract out constant to make 'null' more readable

def NO_TOPIC = null

at least use and: '...' to tell what you are testing  here
but why are you adding this to this test, should it maybe be in separate test, should it really be in this test spec?

actually now I see you have duplicated the previous test. No need to test all scenarios again. YOu can have just one tets here that checks UUID and Topic

this is what I opten manage adding more and more to teh same table :-( 
Instead no nee for all thos otehr column and vartaion you just need to focus on topic here and tets with and without (without ight not even really be needed as it is covered in prev test)
just curious why di you change it, does it make a different or is it just more correct?!
dont think we need  a CI test for this (yet),  what did we agree in the user story?!
this code should be in service layer. No need to handle actual request/reply message
as suggetse before I prefr NOT have to use variables for this and since you changed the assert to use .contains we can inline string literal below 'DataNode not found'
why did you not move these to utility class?
did we agree to add a new test WITH requestId and topic?
I think this is a lot of logic for a ternary operator. Should it be an if else instead?
Same as above, could even be extracted into a separate private method since there is a lot of repetition
extra tabbing, non blocking just harder to see whats being changed

Why did we remove the /.

Was it just unnecessary?
This is only used once below. I think we can just pass null in directly.
Spacing on these can go back one indent.
Spacing here too, but this seems to be legacy issue.
White spacing(I'm very bad for not catching these normally! :) )

There's a test here for data node not found, but it isn't defined in the exception handler class as part of this commit.

Was this done as part of a separate commit because surely this would cause the test to fail?
We have a separate class for constants now, I feel like these should be extracted into that.
Should generated strings be defined as constants?
I think a standard if else statement here would probably be more readable.
If request id is a constant why are we not passing the constants directly in here?

Is it necessary to have this entire class just to set the REQUEST_ID?

Can we not just return it directly?
as per my understanding we had agreed to get this removed

duplicate piece of code
Also can be better readable in conventional if else loop
As we are using pathSegment(DMI_BASE_PATH) "/" not needed.
Yes Its needed I lost it into older patch. reverted back.
It should be not be constent. I changed it.
Used Map to generate response and directly set the request id.
NB: it is a big commit would be could to list a few keypoint here
see previous comments (patch #8) (aslo form Aditya has given feedback)
why did signature change, this is not used, is it an (optional?) framework change?!
name is misleading but I think variable is not needed anyway and this avoid naming-difficulties, just use string literal as I suggested before

these are useful constantst but not sure if they belong in the DMiRegistry specific files. 
I think NO_TOPIC can be on the public interface itself

why all these constants now?! In general I don't agree with the (very old anit-)pattern of introducing constants for each String literal. 
Only if the string literal need further clarification or is likely to change 

in any case not all of these constants are related to DmiRegistry!!!
Because we use the same value everytime here, we can just pass this in as a literal string, and remove this column
this one might be OK but not right place
should NOT fall back to default it HAS to be configured
no need to verify Java library (not written by us)
separate test, can combine blank and invalid topic-if

(*_) any a number of any parameters, not great for positive test!
(_,_,_) exactly 3 parameters of any type
(p1,_p3) parameter 1 ha to  match p1, parameter 2 can be anything, p3 has to match parameter
a dmi datastore URI is generated

as per prod code there should be no handling of this, no need for test 
(maybe just assert the 'configured path' is used)
no variation, need need for column

call it something unique: dmiBaseForTest
then you can asset it is used
We decided to use string literal here.
Did we agree to check if the topic exists in Kafka or is that coming later?
Javadoc does not match method name populateUriVariables
"given" and "and" are very similar in these tests. Could be extracted to out so minimize code and avoid mistakes if things need to be changed.
the created dmi service url matches the expected
perfect, this is teh right level of info in teh commit message

extract out 
expectedRequestId= null
expectedTopicName = null
aqlqys same response, so no need for column

there is no need to repeating that have been tested before it also distracts from what is really being tested here. 
I have re-written this tets locally like this:


  def 'Get resource data for data operational from DMI with valid topic i.e. async request.'() {
        given: 'cps data service returns valid data node'
            mockCpsDataService.getDataNode(*_) >> dataNode
        and: 'dmi data operation returns valid response and data'
            mockDmiDataOperations.getResourceDataFromDmi(_, _,_, _, _ , _, 'my-topic-name')
                    >> new ResponseEntity<>('{result-json}', HttpStatus.OK)
        when: 'get resource data is called for data operational with valid topic'
            def responseData = objectUnderTest.getResourceDataOperationalForCmHandle('','','','','my-topic-name')
        then: 'non empty request id is generated'
            assert responseData.requestId.length()>0
    }

I think this is much clearer, can you apply the same to all the new tests you added (there are some legacy issue related to this as well  I will take care of that later myself, once your commit is finished)

extract out 
expectedRequestId= null
expectedTopicName = null
logically I think this should come before line 85
consider using a real instance, it a simpel util class means less mock-code more test-coverage (even if duplicated) of real code without additional test code line
if one or most tets need this setup it up in setup of static as part of whole test spec
why mock it? Just use the real thing!
It will be coming later. Right now it would be overhead to check if topic exists into kafka.
not suer if this is correct in teh case of async response, we only return a request id....
especially becasue these 'expectations' are not set/dont change in the scope of these test I woudl prefer NO_REQUEST_ID and NO_TOPIC here
As per my discussion with Joe we returns only request id in this case.
no need to test these again

I downloaded the patch in my IDE and I now realise you should NOT use handleResponse() method here. it was intended to handle the response from the dmiDataOperation (on line 329)

just build a body around the requestId and status 200 instead!
can use empty input values like on line 249
At this point we have received the request and are returning the id for retrieval later from kafka
NB - I think we should still have this as a constant shared definition for consistency.

NB - Does the team feel like this could be extracted out as a shared def?

It's hard to tell exactly what a=b is. If was extracted out into a constant, it could be defined in more plain English as an optionsParam?
Same comment as before, should these be extracted into a shared definition.
Is this invalid? I think the URL may still work with an extra /. If not should an exception be thrown, or should we expect a bad request of some sort to test for.

With not necessary as it is already in the test definition. So the label would look like this

'Test dmi api base path with With /'
We used it as constant in previous patch but later decised to use string literal.
Yes it's invalid as if we append base path with "/" then it produces //dmi that is invalid and you will get 404.
NB: Next time let's remove the redundant annotations as agreed earlier by team
same for all no need for column
updated https://jira.onap.org/browse/CPS-588 to follow this up
